summary: Tested server.py modularization into routers (auth, housekeeping, departments), i18n completion for HousekeepingDetailedReports.js, and frontend functionality. Fixed duplicate route conflict in comprehensive_modules_endpoints.py that was causing 404 errors.

minor.issue: Returns plain list instead of wrapped object with 'blocks' and 'count'. Inconsistent response format.

fixed.issue: Was returning 404 due to duplicate route in comprehensive_modules_endpoints.py with super_admin restriction. Removed duplicate MOCK endpoints.
Same duplicate route issue as above - fixed by removing mock endpoints from comprehensive_modules_endpoints.py

issues: Minor: Section headers 'Do Not Disturb (DND) Rooms', 'Sleep Out (SO)', 'Out of Order (OOO)' are hardcoded English instead of using t() - these include industry-standard abbreviations so may be intentional
'Quick Actions:', 'New Booking', 'New Guest', 'Flash Report', 'Refresh' buttons not translated in Turkish mode

action_items: Consider adding i18n for HousekeepingDetailedReports section headers (DND, SO, OOO) - LOW priority as abbreviations are industry standard
Quick Actions buttons in PMS page need i18n translation keys
critical_code_review_comments: comprehensive_modules_endpoints.py had duplicate MOCK implementations of housekeeping endpoints that shadowed the real implementations in routers/housekeeping.py
Router modularization successful - auth.py, housekeeping.py, departments.py properly extracted and working
i18n implementation in HousekeepingDetailedReports.js is correct with useTranslation hook and t() function
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_router_modularization.py - Created comprehensive test suite for extracted routers

success_rate.frontend: 95% - main flows working, minor i18n gaps in headers

context_for_next_testing_agent: Server.py modularization complete. Auth, housekeeping, and department endpoints extracted to routers/. HousekeepingDetailedReports i18n working in Turkish. Minor untranslated strings remain in Quick Actions section and DND/SO/OOO headers.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: comprehensive_modules_endpoints.py contained duplicate route definitions (/housekeeping/room-status-report, /housekeeping/staff-performance-detailed) with MOCK data that were registered before the extracted housekeeping router. These routes had super_admin dependency which returned 404 for non-super-admin users. Solution: Removed the duplicate MOCK routes from comprehensive_modules_endpoints.py, allowing the real implementations in routers/housekeeping.py to be matched.
